items.title: Why pacman may warn broadcom-wl-dkms local is newer than extra
items.summary: If you have the optional cachyos repository enabled and see a warning like warning: broadcom-wl-dkms: local (6.30.223.271-49) is newer than extra (6.30.223.271-48), this is expected and not a bug. cachyos rebuilds some packages — including DKMS driver packages like broadcom-wl-dkms — on its own schedule, and can ship a higher pkgrel than the current extra build before Arch's own rebuild catches up. Kiro's /etc/pacman.conf lists extra before cachyos, so once you're on the cachyos build, later syncs keep resolving against extra's (older) pkgrel and pacman flags the mismatch rather than silently downgrading you. There is nothing to fix — pacman will not downgrade an installed package on its own, so your working driver stays installed. The warning clears itself once extra publishes a pkgrel that catches up or exceeds cachyos's. If you'd rather not see it, you can pin the package to extra with a [options] NoUpgrade = broadcom-wl-dkms line, or simply ignore the warning — it's cosmetic.

items.title: New app: Fish Tweak Tool
items.summary: Say hello to Fish Tweak Tool — a GTK4 graphical configurator for the fish shell. Fish already ships a theme picker, but it can't install prompts and plugins for you. This app fills that gap: install a modern prompt (Tide, Starship, Hydro, Pure), toggle the must-have fisher plugins, browse colour themes, and tune the greeting and cursor — all from one desktop app, no browser, no sudo. It only ever writes per-user overrides to ~/.config/fish/, so it never fights the shipped Kiro defaults. Install it with sudo pacman -S fish-tweak-tool.

items.title: Check your /etc/pacman.conf has the Kiro repositories
items.summary:
Make sure your /etc/pacman.conf enables the two repositories Kiro relies on — nemesis_repo (Kiro's own packages) and chaotic-aur. A third, cachyos, is optional and shipped disabled. If any are missing, open the file as root and add these blocks:
[nemesis_repo]
Include = /etc/pacman.d/kiro-mirrorlist

[chaotic-aur]
Include = /etc/pacman.d/chaotic-mirrorlist

#[cachyos]
#Include = /etc/pacman.d/cachyos-mirrorlist
Then refresh with sudo pacman -Syu. cachyos is shown commented out — it is disabled by default on Kiro (chaotic-aur is the backstop); uncomment it only if you want it. Fresh Kiro installs already have these set up — this is a reminder for anyone who edited pacman.conf by hand or is migrating an existing system.

items.title: Optional: pause upgrades on Arch news with informant
items.summary: Want your system to pause before an upgrade until you've read the latest Arch news? informant is an optional tool that shows Arch announcements and holds back pacman/paru/yay until you've seen them — a useful safety net, especially after incidents like the recent AUR one. It is opt-in and not installed by default on Kiro. To turn it on: paru -S informant (it's in chaotic-aur, already enabled on Kiro), then run informant read to clear the current news. You can remove it any time with paru -R informant.

items.title: Kiro is not affected by the AUR malicious-packages incident
items.summary: Arch has posted a security advisory about a wave of malicious package uploads to the AUR. Kiro is not affected: its installer and tooling install packages only from the official Arch repositories and from chaotic-aur (a signed, centrally-built binary repo). Nothing Kiro ships pulls or builds packages straight from the AUR, and we audited our install scripts after the advisory with no exposure. If you install AUR packages yourself with yay, paru, or by hand, the usual Arch advice applies: review every PKGBUILD and .install script before you build, especially right now. The AUR is user-submitted and unsigned, so that trust step is always yours. There is nothing you need to do on Kiro itself.
